summary.intent: Implement Matrix OIDC authentication (Issue #711) to support modern Matrix homeservers using Matrix Authentication Service (MAS/MSC3861), since matrix.org and others disabled password auth in April 2025.
summary.outcome: OIDC auth mode implemented end-to-end: config types, two-phase browser flow (start/complete RPCs), session persistence, token refresh, UI modals, callback routing, and cross-signing ownership bootstrap. PR #730 created and merged to main. Numerous runtime bugs fixed iteratively through live testing against matrix.org MAS.

repo: ChannelPlugin trait methods must be overridden in `impl ChannelPlugin for MatrixPlugin` — inherent methods on `MatrixPlugin` are not visible through `dyn ChannelPlugin` in the gateway
The registry's `account_index` is only updated via `start_account()` — OIDC bypasses this, requiring explicit `index_account()` calls after oauth_complete/oauth_start to make retry_ownership work
channel status uses `ch.status === 'connected'` not `ch.connected` — the RPC response shape uses a `status` string field
RPC response payload is at `res.payload` not `res.result` — consistent across all channel RPC calls
Existing cross-signing on matrix.org MAS requires browser approval URL (`?action=org.matrix.cross_signing_reset`) before a new device can take over — handled via `CrossSigningResetAuthType::OAuth`
`matrix_auth().logged_in()` returns false for OIDC sessions; use `client.user_id().is_some()` instead which works for both auth methods
ChannelEvent enum uses `#[serde(tag = 'kind', rename_all = 'snake_case')]` — new variants serialize automatically with `kind` field
The existing `/auth/callback` route (not `/api/oauth/callback`) handles OAuth callbacks — three-fallback chain: provider → MCP → channel
Ownership bootstrap runs inside `sync_once_and_spawn_loop` after initial sync — emitting a `StatusChanged` ChannelEvent is the correct way to push real-time ownership updates to the UI

code.finding: `state.client.matrix_auth().logged_in()` only works for password auth; OIDC sessions need `client.user_id().is_some()`
redirect_uri must be normalized from https://localhost to http://localhost for MAS registration (RFC 8252 loopback); client_uri must be a real HTTPS project URL, not localhost
resolve_channel_type() uses account_index which is only populated via start_account() — OIDC flow needs explicit index_account() call
oauth_start originally built hardcoded config with only homeserver/auth_mode/access_token, dropping ownership_mode and all other form fields — must pass full config from JS
Ownership card logic used `auth_mode !== 'password'` as catch-all for 'access token' message — OIDC falls through to wrong message; needs explicit OIDC branch
`transition: opacity 0.15s` on provider-btn caused ghost text — when button text changes simultaneously with disabled state, browser composites old and new text during opacity transition

workflow: Safari renders ghost text when a text node inside <button> changes while an opacity transition is active — fix by keying the button element on its state so Preact destroys/recreates the DOM node rather than patching text in place
HTM/Preact template expressions with nested ternaries can create multiple text nodes; extracting to a helper function and wrapping in <span> avoids this
Light-on-light color patterns (e.g. text-sky-100 on bg-sky-500/10, text-emerald-100 on bg-emerald-500/10) are unreadable in light mode — use bg-*-50 with text-*-900 for tinted info banners
For async ownership setup that completes after the RPC returns, push a WebSocket event (ChannelEvent::StatusChanged) rather than using setTimeout delays in the frontend
GPG-signed commits with YubiKey require interactive terminal presence — background bash tasks time out waiting for the YubiKey touch

friction: YubiKey GPG signing repeatedly blocked background git commit/push tasks — required multiple manual taps and retries throughout the session

Went through 5+ iterations fixing the redirect_uri/client_uri for MAS: https→http normalization, wrong callback path (/api/oauth/callback vs /auth/callback), removing normalization that broke things again
channel status showed 'connected' before ownership bootstrap completed — required multiple approaches (setTimeout delay, then proper WebSocket StatusChanged event) before landing on the right fix
retry_ownership failed with 'unknown channel account' because the registry account_index wasn't updated after OIDC flow — root cause took multiple log sessions to identify
cross-signing bootstrap state is inconsistent across sessions: sometimes succeeds without approval, sometimes requires browser approval URL, depending on prior session state on MAS
Compilation errors from matrix-sdk types (RoomLoadSettings, Raw::new, Localized::new) required multiple iterations to resolve correct import paths
The `m.secret_storage.default_key` deserialization warnings appear on every sync — from a corrupted/incomplete secret storage state left by prior test sessions, not actionable
open_items: OIDC channels are not properly restored from the store on Moltis restart — `start_account` with OIDC mode tries `restore_oidc_session` but if the handle expired or session is stale, the channel may not restart cleanly
E2E tests are UI-only mocks — no integration tests against a real MAS instance; endpoint path mismatches and MAS policy rejections can't be caught by current test suite

Cross-signing ownership bootstrap for moltis_owned OIDC requires browser approval on every new device (each remove+reconnect) — MAS design constraint, but UX could be improved by detecting and reusing existing device keys when possible
Duplicate Matrix account guard (same user_id) is basic — doesn't handle the case of connecting the same account with different auth modes, or provide a merge/upgrade path
The `m.secret_storage.default_key` deserialization failures suggest the test account has corrupted secret storage state that should be cleaned up
No re-authenticate button implemented for OIDC channels in EditMatrixModal (was in the plan but deferred)
channels-matrix.spec.js E2E tests are UI-only with mocked RPCs — should be extended to test actual OIDC flow with a test MAS instance when available
